title: Conditional distributions
description: Practice calculating conditional distributions in two-way tables.

assessment_items.question_content:
James is interested in the relationship between weather conditions and whether the downtown train he sometimes takes runs on time. For a year, James records whether each day is sunny, cloudy, rainy, or snowy, as well as whether this train arrives on time or is delayed. His results are displayed in the table below.

Weather condition|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

**Give the conditional distribution of arrival status for cloudy days.**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth of a percent.*

 | On time  | Delayed
- | - 
Cloudy |  [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\%$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]  $\%$
hints:
##Identify the distribution

In this question, the condition is the cloudy weather and the variable whose distribution we are looking for is whether or not the train is on time.

|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $\boxed{115}$ | $\boxed{5}$ | $\boxed{120}$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

##Calculate the percents

There are a total of $120$ cloudy days, $115$ of which the train was on time and $5$ of which it was delayed. If we select a cloudy day at random, we get the following probabilities.

$P(\text{on time}|\text{cloudy})=\dfrac{115}{120}\approx 95.8\%$

$P(\text{delayed}|\text{cloudy})= \dfrac{5}{120}\approx 4.2\%$

##Answer

|On time|Delayed
- | - | -
Cloudy| $ 95.8\%$ | $ 4.2\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
Leon is interested in the relationships among geographic region and political affiliation. Leon collected data about the political parties of US senators at that time and the regions of the US that they represent. His results are in the table below.

Region | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $20$ | $0$ | $26$
Southwest | $1$ | $5$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $10$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $11$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $14$ | $7$ | $1$ | $22$
Pacific | $2$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $55$ | $1$ | $100$

**Give the conditional distribution of political party for the northeast region.**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth of a percent.*

 | Democrats  | Republicans | Independents
- | - 
Northeast|  [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\%$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]  $\%$ | [[☃ numeric-input 5]]  $\%$
hints:
##Identify the distribution

In this question, the condition is the northeast region and the variable whose distribution we are looking for is political party.

 | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $20$ | $0$ | $26$
Southwest | $1$ | $5$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $10$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $11$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $\boxed{14}$ | $\boxed{7}$ | $\boxed{1}$ | $\boxed{22}$
Pacific | $2$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $55$ | $1$ | $100$

##Calculate the percents

There are a total of $22$ senators representing the northeast region, $14$ of whom are in the Democratic Party, $7$ of whom are in the Republican Party, and $1$ of whom is in the Independent Party. If we select a senator representing the northeast region at random, we get the following probabilities.

$P(\text{Democrat}|\text{northeast})=\dfrac{14}{22}\approx 63.6\%$

$P(\text{Republican}|\text{northeast})=\dfrac{7}{22}\approx 31.8\%$

$P(\text{Independent}|\text{northeast})=\dfrac{1}{22}\approx 4.5\%$

##Answer


|Democrats|Republicans|Independents
- | - | -
Northeast| $ 63.6\%$| $31.8\%$ | $ 4.5\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table gives the number of people on the Titanic by class and whether or not they survived.

Class | Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $202$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $118$ | $167$ | $285$
Third class | $178$ | $528$ | $706$
Crew class | $215$ | $698$ | $913$
Total | $713$ | $1516$ | $2229$

[http://www.historyonthenet.com/Titanic/passengers.htm]: (Source)

**Give the conditional distribution of whether or not the person survived for the second class.**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth of a percent.*

 | Survived | Died
- | - 
Second class|  [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\%$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]  $\%$
hints:
##Identify the distribution

In this question, the condition is being in second class and the variable whose distribution we are looking for is whether or not the person survived.

 | Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $202$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $\boxed{118}$ | $\boxed{167}$ | $\boxed{285}$
Third class | $178$ | $528$ | $706$
Crew class | $215$ | $698$ | $913$
Total | $713$ | $1516$ | $2229$

##Calculate the percents

There are a total of $285$ people in the second class, $118$ of whom survived and $167$ of whom died. If we select a person from the second class at random, we get the following probabilities.

$P(\text{survived}|\text{second class})=\dfrac{118}{285}\approx 41.4\%$

$P(\text{died}|\text{second class})=\dfrac{167}{285}\approx 58.6\%$

##Answer


 | Survived | Died
-| -| -|-
Second class | $41.4\%$ | $58.6\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
Lara surveyed all of the $11^{\text{th}}$ grade students at her school about the number of languages they speak and whether or not they have allergies. Here are her results:

Languages | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $25$ | $28$ | $53$
$2$ languages | $19$ | $14$ | $33$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $1$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $1$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $44$ | $91$

**Give the conditional distribution of whether or not the person has allergies for the students who speak $2$ languages.**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth of a percent.*

 | Allergies | No Allergies
-|-|-|-
$2$ languages |  [[☃ numeric-input 3]] $\%$ | [[☃ numeric-input 4]]  $\%$
hints:
##Identify the distribution

In this question, the condition is speaking $2$ languages and the variable whose distribution we are looking for is whether or not the student has allergies.

 | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $25$ | $28$ | $53$
$2$ languages | $\boxed{19}$ | $\boxed{14}$ | $\boxed{33}$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $1$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $1$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $44$ | $91$

##Calculate the percents

There are a total of $33$ students who speak $2$ languages, $19$ of whom have allergies and $14$ of whom have no allergies. If we select a student who speaks $2$ languages at random, we get the following probabilities.

$P(\text{allergies}|\text{2 languages})=\dfrac{19}{33}\approx 57.6\%$

$P(\text{no allergies}|\text{2 languages})=\dfrac{14}{33}\approx 42.4\%$

##Answer


 | Allergies | No Allergies
-|-|-|-
$2$ languages | $57.6\%$ | $42.4\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
James is interested in the relationship between weather conditions and whether the downtown train he sometimes takes runs on time. For a year, James records whether each day is sunny, cloudy, rainy, or snowy, as well as whether this train arrives on time or is delayed. His results are displayed in the table below.

Weather condition|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

**Give the conditional distribution of weather condition for delayed trains.**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth of a percent.*

|Delayed|
- | - 
Sunny| [[☃ numeric-input 5]] $\%$
Cloudy | [[☃ numeric-input 6]] $\%$ 
Rainy | [[☃ numeric-input 7]] $\%$
Snowy | [[☃ numeric-input 8]] $\%$
hints:
##Identify the distribution

In this question, the condition is the delayed train and the variable whose distribution we are looking for is kind of weather.

|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $\boxed{3}$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $\boxed{5}$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $\boxed{15}$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $\boxed{12}$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $\boxed{35}$ | $365$

##Calculate the percents

There are a total of $35$ delayed trains, $3$ on sunny days, $5$ on cloudy days, $15$ on rainy days, and $12$ on snowy days. If we select a day when the train was delayed at random, we get the following probabilities.

$P(\text{sunny}|\text{delayed})=\dfrac{3}{35}\approx 8.6\%$

$P(\text{cloudy}|\text{delayed})=\dfrac{5}{35}\approx 14.3\%$

$P(\text{rainy}|\text{delayed})=\dfrac{15}{35}\approx 42.9\%$

$P(\text{snowy}|\text{delayed})=\dfrac{12}{35}\approx 34.3\%$

##Answer


|Delayed|
- | - 
Sunny| $ 8.6\%$ 
Cloudy| $ 14.3\%$ 
Rainy| $ 42.9\%$ 
Snowy| $ 34.3\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
Leon is interested in the relationships among geographic region and political affiliation. Leon collected data about the political parties of US senators at that time and the regions of the US that they represent. His results are in the table below.

Region | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $20$ | $0$ | $26$
Southwest | $1$ | $5$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $10$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $11$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $14$ | $7$ | $1$ | $22$
Pacific | $2$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $55$ | $1$ | $100$

**Give the conditional distribution of region for senators in the Republican Party.**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth of a percent.*

|Republicans|
- | - 
Southeast| [[☃ numeric-input 5]] $\%$
Southwest| [[☃ numeric-input 6]] $\%$ 
West| [[☃ numeric-input 7]] $\%$
Midwest| [[☃ numeric-input 8]] $\%$
Northeast | $12.7\%$
Pacific | $3.6\%$
hints:
##Identify the distribution

In this question, the condition is being in the Republican Party and the variable whose distribution we are looking for is region the senator represents.

 | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $\boxed{20}$ | $0$ | $26$
Southwest | $1$ | $\boxed{5}$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $\boxed{10}$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $\boxed{11}$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $14$ | $\boxed{7}$ | $1$ | $22$
Pacific | $2$ | $\boxed{2}$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $\boxed{55}$ | $1$ | $100$

##Calculate the percents

There are a total of $55$ senators in the Republican Party, with $20$ representing the southeast, $5$ representing the southwest, $10$ representing the west, and $11$ representing the midwest. If we select a senator in the Republican Party at random, we get the following probabilities.

$P(\text{southeast}|\text{Republican})=\dfrac{20}{55}\approx 36.4\%$

$P(\text{southwest}|\text{Republican})=\dfrac{5}{55}\approx 9.1\%$

$P(\text{west}|\text{Republican})=\dfrac{10}{55}\approx 18.2\%$

$P(\text{midwest}|\text{Republican})=\dfrac{11}{55}= 20\%$

##Answer


|Republican|
- | - 
Southeast | $36.4\%$
Southwest | $9.1\%$
West | $18.2\%$
Midwest | $20.0\%$
Northeast | $12.7\%$
Pacific | $3.6\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table gives the number of people on the Titanic by class and whether or not they survived.

Class | Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $202$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $118$ | $167$ | $285$
Third class | $178$ | $528$ | $706$
Crew class | $215$ | $698$ | $913$
Total | $713$ | $1516$ | $2229$

[http://www.historyonthenet.com/Titanic/passengers.htm]: (Source)

**Complete the table giving the conditional distribution of class for people who survived.**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth of a percent.*

 | Survived | 
- | - 
First class| [[☃ numeric-input 5]] $\%$
Second class| [[☃ numeric-input 6]] $\%$ 
Third class | [[☃ numeric-input 7]] $\%$
Crew class | [[☃ numeric-input 8]] $\%$
hints:
##Identify the distribution

In this question, the condition is having survived and the variable whose distribution we are looking for is the person's class.

 | Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $\boxed{202}$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $\boxed{118}$ | $167$ | $285$
Third class | $\boxed{178}$ | $528$ | $706$
Crew class | $\boxed{215}$ | $698$ | $913$
Total | $\boxed{713}$ | $1516$ | $2229$

##Calculate the percents

There are a total of $713$ people who survived, with $202$ from the first class, $118$ from the second class, $178$ from the third class, and $215$ from the crew. If we select a person who survived at random, we get the following probabilities.

$P(\text{first class}|\text{survived})=\dfrac{202}{713}\approx 28.3\%$

$P(\text{second class}|\text{survived})=\dfrac{118}{713}\approx 16.5\%$

$P(\text{third class}|\text{survived})=\dfrac{178}{713}\approx 25.0\%$

$P(\text{crew class}|\text{survived})=\dfrac{215}{713}\approx 30.2\%$

##Answer


 | Survived | 
-| -
First class | $28.3\%$
Second class |$16.5\%$
Third class | $25\%$
Crew class | $30.2\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
Lara surveyed all of the $11^{\text{th}}$ grade students at her school about the number of languages they speak and whether or not they have allergies. Here are her results:

Languages  | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $25$ | $28$ | $53$
$2$ languages | $19$ | $14$ | $33$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $1$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $1$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $44$ | $91$

**Complete the table giving the conditional distribution of number of languages spoken for students with no allergies.**  
*Round your answers to the nearest tenth of a percent.*

 | No allergies| 
- | - 
$1$ language | [[☃ numeric-input 5]] $\%$
$2$ languages | [[☃ numeric-input 6]] $\%$ 
$3$ languages | [[☃ numeric-input 7]] $\%$
$6$ languages | [[☃ numeric-input 8]] $\%$
hints:
##Identify the distribution

In this question, the condition is having no allergies and the variable whose distribution we are looking for is number of languages spoken.


 | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $25$ | $\boxed{28}$ | $53$
$2$ languages | $19$ | $\boxed{14}$ | $33$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $\boxed{1}$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $\boxed{1}$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $\boxed{44}$ | $91$

##Calculate the percents

There are a total of $44$ students with no allergies, with $28$ who speak $1$ language, $14$ who speak $2$ languages, $1$ who speaks $3$ languages, and $1$ who speaks $6$ languages. If we select a student with no allergies at random, we get the following probabilities.

$P(\text{1 language}|\text{no allergies})=\dfrac{28}{44}\approx 63.6\%$

$P(\text{2 languages}|\text{no allergies})=\dfrac{14}{44}\approx 31.8\%$

$P(\text{3 languages}|\text{no allergies})=\dfrac{1}{44}\approx 2.3\%$

$P(\text{6 languages}|\text{no allergies})=\dfrac{1}{44}\approx 2.3\%$

##Answer


| No Allergies | 
-|-
$1$ language | $63.6\%$
$2$ languages | $31.8\%$
$3$ languages | $2.3\%$
$6$ languages | $2.3\%$

assessment_items.question_content:
James is interested in the relationship between weather conditions and whether the downtown train he sometimes takes runs on time. For a year, James records whether each day is sunny, cloudy, rainy, or snowy, as well as whether this train arrives on time or is delayed. His results are displayed in the table below.

Weather condition|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

**Which graph displays the conditional distributions of arrival status for each weather condition?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The following graph shows the conditional distributions of arrival status for each weather condition.

![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus weather condition. There are four bars representing sunny, cloudy, rainy, and snowy, respectively. Each bar extends from 0 to 100 percent and is divided into two parts. The lower represents, delayed, and the upper represents, on-time. Sunny is divided at 1 percent. Cloudy is divided at 3 percent. Rainy is divided at 27 percent. Snowy is divided at 60 percent. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ebb4d0a8113bd7e4809d8821d2af51fc1d68c3b)

We want the graph that shows the conditional distributions of arrival status for each weather condition. So we need a bar for each weather condition that shows what percent of trains were on-time and what percent were delayed for that weather condition.

choices.content: ![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus weather condition. There are four bars representing sunny, cloudy, rainy, and snowy, respectively. Each bar extends from 0 to 100 percent and is divided into two parts. The lower represents, delayed, and the upper represents, on-time. Sunny is divided at 1 percent. Cloudy is divided at 3 percent. Rainy is divided at 27 percent. Snowy is divided at 60 percent. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ebb4d0a8113bd7e4809d8821d2af51fc1d68c3b)

![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus status. There are two bars representing on-time and delayed, respectively. Each bar extends from 0 to 100 percent and is divided into four parts. From bottom to top, the sections represent weather conditions snowy, rainy, cloudy, and sunny. Both on-time and delayed have the following, identical percentages for each weather condition. Snowy, 1 percent. Rainy, 16 percent. Cloudy, 31 percent. Sunny, 52 percent. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3b1c3d35dc15c74a7a67289e27b45dfad31c8019)

assessment_items.question_content:
Leon is interested in the relationships among geographic region and political affiliation. Leon collected data about the political parties of US senators at that time and the regions of the US that they represent. His results are in the table below.

Region | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $20$ | $0$ | $26$
Southwest | $1$ | $5$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $10$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $11$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $14$ | $7$ | $1$ | $22$
Pacific | $2$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $55$ | $1$ | $100$

**Which graph displays the conditional distributions of region for each political party?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We want the graph that shows the conditional distributions of region for each political party. So we need a bar for each political party that shows what percent of senators were from each region for that political party.

The following graph displays the conditional distributions of region for each political party.

![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus political party. There are three bars representing Democrats, Republicans, and Independents, respectively. Each bar extends from 0 to 100 percent and is divided into six parts. From bottom to top, the sections represent regions Pacific, Northeast, Midwest, West, Southwest, Southeast. By political party, the percentages for each region are as follows. Democrats. Pacific, 4. Northeast, 34. Midwest, 24. West, 20. Southwest, 3. Southeast, 15. Republicans. Pacific, 3. Northeast, 16. Midwest, 19. West, 18. Southwest, 7. Southeast, 37. Independents. Northeast, 100. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b65ae8955125c7d6c2eb267ff0cd752cef5ad4ea)

choices.content: ![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus region. There are six bars representing Southeast, Southwest, West, Midwest, Northeast, and Pacific, respectively. Each bar extends from 0 to 100 percent and is divided into three parts. From bottom to top, the sections represent independents, republicans, and democrats. By region, the percentages of each are as follows. Southeast. Independents, 0. Republicans, 79. Democrats, 21. Southwest. Independent, 0. Republicans, 81. Democrats, 19. West. Independents, 0. Republicans, 58. Democrats, 42. Midwest. Independents, 0. Republicans, 42. Democrats, 58. Northeast. Independents, 4. Republicans, 34. Democrats, 62. Pacific. Independents, 0. Republicans, 50. Democrats, 50. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2ce950a6cf11f3b807c8d4d1952fdf5e0cdf9a4a)

![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus political party. There are three bars representing Democrats, Republicans, and Independents, respectively. Each bar extends from 0 to 100 percent and is divided into six parts. From bottom to top, the sections represent regions Pacific, Northeast, Midwest, West, Southwest, Southeast. By political party, the percentages for each region are as follows. Democrats. Pacific, 4. Northeast, 34. Midwest, 24. West, 20. Southwest, 3. Southeast, 15. Republicans. Pacific, 3. Northeast, 16. Midwest, 19. West, 18. Southwest, 7. Southeast, 37. Independents. Northeast, 100. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b65ae8955125c7d6c2eb267ff0cd752cef5ad4ea)

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table gives the number of people on the Titanic by class and whether or not they survived.

 Class| Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $202$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $118$ | $167$ | $285$
Third class | $178$ | $528$ | $706$
Crew class | $215$ | $698$ | $913$
Total | $713$ | $1516$ | $2229$

[http://www.historyonthenet.com/Titanic/passengers.htm]: (Source)

**Which graph displays the conditional distribution of whether a person survived or died for the crew?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: We see that $\dfrac{215}{913}\approx 24\%$ of the crew survived, and $\dfrac{698}{913}\approx 76\%$ of the crew died. So the section of the bar representing the crew who died should be show a relative frequency of $76\%$.

The following graph shows the conditional distribution of whether a person died or not for the crew.

![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus passenger class. There is a single bar representing crew class that extends from 0 to 100 percent. The bar is divided into two parts with the lower part representing, died, and the upper representing, survived. The bar is divided at approximately 76 percent.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c6deefb9d47db3a0736d0c032f5bacde5545e014)

We want the graph that shows the conditional distribution of whether a person survived or died for the crew. So we need a single bar for the crew that shows what percent of the crew survived and what percent died.

choices.content: ![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus passenger class. There is a single bar representing crew class that extends from 0 to 100 percent. The bar is divided into two parts with the lower part representing, died, and the upper representing, survived. The bar is divided at approximately 76 percent.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c6deefb9d47db3a0736d0c032f5bacde5545e014)

![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus passenger class. There is a single bar representing crew class that extends from 0 to 100 percent. The bar is divided into two parts with the lower part representing, died, and the upper representing, survived. The bar is divided at approximately 24 percent.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/b4a132ded12b8eacbc3c5e0fe7b6645d2c9db8ee)

assessment_items.question_content:
Lara surveyed all of the $11^{\text{th}}$ grade students at her school about the number of languages they speak and whether or not they have allergies. Here are her results:

Languages | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $25$ | $28$ | $53$
$2$ languages | $19$ | $14$ | $33$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $1$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $1$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $44$ | $91$

**Which graph displays the conditional distribution of number of languages spoken for students with allergies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The following graph displays the conditional distribution of number of languages spoken for students with allergies.

![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus student. There is a single bar representing allergies that extends from 0 to 100 percent. It is divided into four parts representing number of languages. From bottom to top, the sections represent 6 languages, 3 languages, 2 languages, and 1 language. The percentages for each section are as follows. 6 languages, 1. 3 languages, 2. 2 languages, 41. 1 language, 56. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ff45cc7467a941bacb4895725ff72026cab64d8a)

Let's find the percents of students with allergies that speak each number of languages.

$1$ language: $\dfrac{25}{47} \approx 53\%$

$2$ languages: $\dfrac{19}{47} \approx 40\%$

$3$ languages: $\dfrac{2}{47} \approx 4\%$

$6$ languages: $\dfrac{1}{47} \approx 2\%$

So a little more than half of the bar should represent students who speak $1$ language, and a little less than half of the bar should represent students who speak $2$ languages.

We want the graph that shows the conditional distribution of number of languages spoken for students with allergies. So we need a single bar for the students with allergies that shows what percent of the students speak each number of languages.

choices.content: ![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus student. There is a single bar representing allergies that extends from 0 to 100 percent. It is divided into four parts representing number of languages. From bottom to top, the sections represent 6 languages, 3 languages, 2 languages, and 1 language. The percentages for each section are as follows. 6 languages, 2. 3 languages, 40. 2 languages, 1. 1 language, 57. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6c3399790c520157432f2084ad237fe335f67746)

![A segmented bar graph plots relative frequency versus student. There is a single bar representing allergies that extends from 0 to 100 percent. It is divided into four parts representing number of languages. From bottom to top, the sections represent 6 languages, 3 languages, 2 languages, and 1 language. The percentages for each section are as follows. 6 languages, 1. 3 languages, 2. 2 languages, 41. 1 language, 56. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/ff45cc7467a941bacb4895725ff72026cab64d8a)

assessment_items.question_content:
James is interested in the relationship between weather conditions and whether the downtown train he sometimes takes runs on time. For a year, James records whether each day is sunny, cloudy, rainy, or snowy, as well as whether this train arrives on time or is delayed. His results are displayed in the table below.

Weather condition|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$

**Which of the following interpretations are correct?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
"When the weather was snowy, it was more likely for the train to be delayed than on time" is **correct.**

When the weather was snowy, the train was delayed on $12$ of $20$ days.

|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $\Large8$ | $\Large12$ | $\Large20$
Total| $330$ | $35$ | $365$
"Given the train was on time, the weather was likely sunny or cloudy" is **correct.**

Sunny and cloudy days accounted for $167+115=282$ of the $330$ on time trains.

|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $\Large167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $\Large115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $12$ | $20$
Total| $\Large330$ | $35$ | $365$
"The most delays occurred on snowy days" is **not correct.**

There were more delays that occurred on rainy days.

|On time|Delayed|Total
- | - | - | - | -
Sunny| $167$  | $3$ | $170$
Cloudy| $115$ | $5$ | $120$
Rainy| $40$ | $\Large15$ | $55$
Snowy| $8$ | $\Large12$ | $20$
Total| $330$ | $\Large35$ | $365$
**The answers:**

When the weather is snowy, it is more likely for the train to be delayed than on time.

The train is on time mostly on sunny and cloudy days.

choices.content: When the weather was snowy, it was more likely for the train to be delayed than on time.
Given the train was on time, the weather was likely sunny or cloudy.
The most delays occurred on snowy days.

assessment_items.question_content:
Leon is interested in the relationships among geographic region and political affiliation. Leon collected data about the political parties of US senators at that time and the regions of the US that they represent. His results are in the table below.

Region | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $20$ | $0$ | $26$
Southwest | $1$ | $5$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $10$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $11$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $14$ | $7$ | $1$ | $22$
Pacific | $2$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $55$ | $1$ | $100$

**Which of the following interpretations are correct?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
"Given that a senator was in the Republican Party, the senator was more likely to represent the midwest than any other region" is **not correct.**

If senator was in the Republican Party, then ${5}$ out of ${55}$ times the senator represented the southwest. There were other regions, such as the southeast, whom senators in the Republican Party represented more frequently.

 | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $\Large{20}$ | $0$ | $26$
Southwest | $1$ | $\Large{{5}}$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $10$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $11$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $14$ | $7$ | $1$ | $22$
Pacific | $2$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $\Large{{55}}$ | $1$ | $100$
"Given that a senator is an Independent, it is certain that the senator represented the northeast" is **correct.**

The northeast region accounted for the only Independent senator in the data.

 | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $20$ | $0$ | $26$
Southwest | $1$ | $5$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $10$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $11$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $14$ | $7$ | $\Large{{1}}$ | $22$
Pacific | $2$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $55$ | $\Large{{1}}$ | $100$
"Given that a senator represents the southeast, it is likely that the senator is in the Republican Party" is **correct.**

Senators in the Republican Party account for ${20}$ out of ${26}$ of the senators representing the southeast region.

 | Democrats | Republicans | Independents | Total
-|-|-|-|-
Southeast | $6$ | $\Large{{20}}$ | $0$ | $\Large{{26}}$
Southwest | $1$ | $5$ | $0$ | $6$
West | $8$ | $10$ | $0$ | $18$
Midwest | $13$ | $11$ | $0$ | $24$
Northeast | $14$ | $7$ | $1$ | $22$
Pacific | $2$ | $2$ | $0$ | $4$
Total | $44$ | $55$ | $1$ | $100$
The following interpretations are correct:

- Given that a senator is an Independent, it is certain that the senator represented the northeast.

- Given that a senator represents the southeast, it is likely that the senator is in the Republican Party.

choices.content: Given that a senator was in the Republican Party, the senator was more likely to represent the midwest than any other region.
Given that a senator is an Independent, it is certain that the senator represented the northeast.
Given that a senator represents the southeast, it is likely that the senator is in the Republican Party.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following table gives the number of people on the Titanic by class and whether or not they survived.

Class | Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $202$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $118$ | $167$ | $285$
Third class | $178$ | $528$ | $706$
Crew class | $215$ | $698$ | $913$
Total | $713$ | $1516$ | $2229$

[http://www.historyonthenet.com/Titanic/passengers.htm]: (Source)

**Which of the following interpretations are correct?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
"If a person survived, it is more likely that the person was in the crew than any other individual class" is **correct.**

There were more people who survived, ${215}$ people, from the crew than from any other individual class.

 | Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $202$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $118$ | $167$ | $285$
Third class | $178$ | $528$ | $706$
Crew class | $\Large{{215}}$ | $698$ | $913$
Total | $713$ | $1516$ | $2229$
"Given that a person was in the crew, it was more likely that the person died than survived" is **correct.**

Of people in the crew, $\dfrac{{215}}{{913}}\approx 24\%$ survived, while $\dfrac{{698}}{{913}}\approx 76\%$ died.

 | Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $202$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $118$ | $167$ | $285$
Third class | $178$ | $528$ | $706$
Crew class | $\Large{{215}}$ | $\Large{{698}}$ | $\Large{{913}}$
Total | $713$ | $1516$ | $2229$
"If a person died, it is likely that the person was in the third class or crew" is **correct.**

People from the third class or crew accounted for ${528}+{698}=1226$ of the ${1516}$ people who died. That is approximately $81\%$.

 | Survived | Died | Total
-| -| -|-
First class | $202$ | $123$ | $325$
Second class | $118$ | $167$ | $285$
Third class | $178$ | $\Large{{528}}$ | $706$
Crew class | $215$ | $\Large{{698}}$ | $913$
Total | $713$ | $\Large{{1516}}$ | $2229$
The following interpretations are correct:

- If a person survived, it is more likely that the person was in the crew than any other individual class.

- Given that a person was in the crew, it was more likely that the person died than survived.

- If a person died, it is likely that the person was in the third class or crew.

choices.content: If a person survived, it is more likely that the person was in the crew than any other individual class.
Given that a person was in the crew, it was more likely that the person died than survived.
If a person died, it is likely that the person was in the third class or crew.

assessment_items.question_content:
Lara surveyed all of the $11^{\text{th}}$ grade students at her school about the number of languages they speak and whether or not they have allergies. Here are her results:

Languages | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $25$ | $28$ | $53$
$2$ languages | $19$ | $14$ | $33$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $1$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $1$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $44$ | $91$

**Which of the following interpretations are correct?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
"If a student has no allergies, it is unlikely that the student speaks more than $2$ languages" is **correct.**

Of the $\blueD{44}$ students with no allergies, only $\blueD{1}+\blueD{1}=2$ of them speak more than $2$ languages. That is only about $\dfrac{2}{44}\approx 5\%$ of the students with no allergies.

 | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $25$ | $28$ | $53$
$2$ languages | $19$ | $14$ | $33$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $\Large{{1}}$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $\Large{{1}}$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $\Large{{44}}$ | $91$
"If a student speaks $1$ language, the student is more likely to have allergies than to not have allergies" is **not correct.**

Of students who speak $1$ language, $\dfrac{{25}}{{53}}\approx 47\%$ have allergies, while the other $\dfrac{{28}}{{53}}\approx 53\%$ have no allergies.

 | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $\Large{{25}}$ | $\Large{{28}}$ | $\Large{{53}}$
$2$ languages | $19$ | $14$ | $33$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $1$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $1$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $44$ | $91$
"If a student has no allergies, it is about equally likely that the student speaks $1$ language or more than $1$ language" is **not correct.**

Of the ${44}$ students with no allergies, $\dfrac{{28}}{{44}}\approx 64\%$ speak $1$ language. That means that about $100\%-64\%=36\%$ of the students with no allergies speak more than $1$ language.

 | Allergies | No Allergies | Total
-|-|-|-
$1$ language | $25$ | $\Large{{28}}$ | $53$
$2$ languages | $19$ | $14$ | $33$
$3$ languages | $2$ | $1$ | $3$
$6$ languages | $1$ | $1$ | $2$
Total | $47$ | $\Large{{44}}$ | $91$
The following interpretation is correct:

- If a student has no allergies, it is unlikely that the student speaks more than $2$ languages.

choices.content: If a student has no allergies, it is unlikely that the student speaks more than $2$ languages.
If a student speaks $1$ language, the student is more likely to have allergies than to not have allergies.
If a student has no allergies, it is about equally likely that the student speaks $1$ language or more than $1$ language.
